title: papaver rhoeas f. chelidoniifolium
intro: Papaver rhoeas f. chelidoniifolium, also known as the Field Poppy, is a subspecies of the common poppy (Papaver rhoeas), a flowering plant native to Europe and Asia. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been used to treat pain and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Papaver rhoeas f. chelidoniifolium include its ability to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into tinctures, infusions, or salves, and are often used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments.
